as: Focuses on historical cost with limited revaluation use.
indAs: Cost model or revaluation model, with component accounting.
ifrs: Same broad model as Ind AS.
usGaap: Mostly historical cost, with some limited fair value options.
logicShift: From simple cost tracking to more judgment-based asset measurement.

as: PPE recognized when future economic benefits are probable and cost can be measured reliably.
At cost, including purchase price, non-refundable taxes, freight, installation, and directly attributable costs.
logicShift: Cost includes all directly attributable costs.

as: Usually carried at cost less depreciation and impairment.
usGaap: Cost less depreciation and impairment; revaluation model generally not allowed for PPE.
logicShift: Ind AS/IFRS allow fair value uplift through revaluation.

as: Based on useful life and residual value; component accounting less emphasized historically.
usGaap: Component accounting is also required where appropriate.
logicShift: More precise matching of cost to consumption.
Estimates are updated more actively under Ind AS/IFRS.

usGaap: Reviewed periodically, usually when indicators change.
logicShift: More frequent reassessment under Ind AS/IFRS.

usGaap: ASC 360: recoverability test, then impairment loss if carrying value exceeds fair value.
logicShift: Different impairment mechanics can affect timing and size of losses.

as: Generally capitalized when directly attributable to qualifying assets.
indAs: Capitalize directly attributable borrowing costs during construction.
logicShift: Construction assets often carry interest into cost.

as: Often recognized based on specific guidance, sometimes netted against asset cost.
indAs: IAS 20-style treatment: deferred income or deduction from asset cost depending on policy.
logicShift: Grant accounting affects opening PPE carrying value.

indAs: Certain FX differences on foreign currency borrowings may be capitalized in some cases.
usGaap: FX treatment follows US GAAP foreign currency rules.
logicShift: Capitalization of FX is more limited and rule-based.

indAs: Allowed for classes of PPE; revaluation surplus goes to OCI.
logicShift: Major divergence between Ind AS/IFRS and US GAAP.
